title: I run a $10M ARR startup with 2 employees and AI
post:
$10M ARR. Two employees. Me and one contractor.

That's not a humble brag — it's a data point about what's possible now.

AI handles our customer support, our marketing copy, our data analysis, our meeting summaries. The contractor handles things AI can't legally do yet.

I've run startups with 50-person teams. I've raised VC money and built "proper" infrastructure. This is different.

We're profitable. We're shipping faster than I ever did with a full team. And I'm working 35 hours a week instead of 80.

The playbook has changed. You don't need to choose between moving fast and being lean. You can have both — if you're willing to rethink everything.

Who else is running something similar? Thoughts?
